rulings.Case Title: GENE LEPORE VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Navistar, Inc'S Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 21, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Navistar, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication: As to Issue One (third cause of action), a hearing is required. As to Issue Two (punitive damages), the motion is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The deposition testimony of James Shuman (Exhibits O and Y), the caution label (Exhibit S), and the service manual (Exhibit BB) create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. The Court will not consider plaintiffs' late-filed March 20, 2017 opposition papers. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/CML)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 21, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs' motion for order to apportion and distribute funds will be heard at 9:00a.m. in Department 514, Hon. Stephen M. Murphy presiding. Henceforth, all asbestos apportionment motions will be heard in Department 514 and such motions shall be filed in accordance with San Francisco Uniform Local Rules of Court, Rule 8.1(B)(5). = (503/CML)
